calendar.matters:
Notice And Motion To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Discovery And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-13-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, tentative ruling is adopted. PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC.'s Motion To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Discovery is granted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Defendant is ordered to respond within 15 days of notice of this order. Sanctions are awarded in the amount of $340, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Prevailing party to submit form of order. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.

SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Mar-01-2010 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned from the Presiding Judge Dept. 206 for Court Trial/Prove-Up Hearing. Comm: William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Lois Jones, CSR #3689.

Douglas B. Provencher, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Creditors Trade Association, Inc., dba Great Western Collection Bureau.
Ronald Ableman, credit manager for plaintiff, was placed under oath and examined.
No appearnce by defendant Narinder Mahal, individually as guarantor of Kanar Enterprises, Inc.
Default was entered as to defendant Kanar Enterprises, inc., a California corporation, on May 8, 2009.
Dismissal without prejudice as to defendant Kiran J. Nahal, a/k/a Keran J. Mahal, individually, was filed on November 25, 2008.
Doe defendants were dismissed today, March 1, 2010.
Notice of Time and Place of Trial was filed on July 22, 2009.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Creditors Trade Association, Inc., dba Great Western Collection Bureau, and against defendants Narinder Mahal, individually as guarantor of Kanar Enterproses, Inc., and Kanar Enterproses, Inc., a California corporation, as follows:

Principal - $14,514.29.
Interest - $4,646.84.
Attorney's Fees - Reserved.
Costs - $635.00.
Sanctions (per Order issued September 2, 2009) - $290.00.

Total - $20,086.13.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-01-2010 Assigned To Trial Court

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Oct-28-2009 Continued To Mar-01-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Oct-28-2009 Text Ruling

Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Attendance At Deposition And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-18-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, tentative ruling is adopted. Plaintiff Creditors Trade association, Inc.'s Motion to compel attendance at deposition and for award of monetary sanctions granted:
1. Defendant Narinder Mahal shall appear for deposition, and produce documents as set forth in the notice of deposition, within twenty days of notice of entry of this order, at a place, date, and time to be determined by plaintiff, or as otherwise agreed by the parties;
2. Sanctions shall issue in the sum of $290.00, payable within thirty days of notice of entry of this order. Prevailing party to submit form of order. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-22-2009 Set For Trial On Nov-02-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents; Request For Admissions And Responses To Pltf'S First Set Of Special Interrogatories; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-20-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. Plaintiff Creditors Trade association, Inc.'s Motion to compel response to request for production of documents; request for admissions and response to plaintiff's first set of special interrogatories granted as follows:
a. All objections are waived, except for attorney-client and attorney work product privilege;
b. Within seven days of the date of service of this order, defendant shall serve full, complete and verified responses to plaintiff's request for production of documents (set one); request for admissions (set one); and first set of special interrogatories;
c. Request for monetary sanctions granted; sanctions shall issue in the sum of $290.00, payable within 30 days. Prevailing party to submit form of order. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.
